skill.name: slide-making-skill
skill.description: Create and verify single-slide PowerPoint files with PptxGenJS, including layout, typography, images, charts, tables, and export checks.
skill.summary: Guides native PowerPoint slide creation with PptxGenJS and practical QA checks.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found the files are Markdown guidance and PptxGenJS examples, not executable malware. Most external-command and weak-crypto findings are false positives from code fences, inline backticks, file extensions, and API names. The remaining low risks are legitimate command examples for QA and placeholder remote image URLs.

low_findings.title: Local command examples in documentation
low_findings.description: The skill documents npm installation and QA commands. These are legitimate for generating and checking slides, but users should review commands before running them. Verdict: true low-risk operational behavior. Confidence: 0.86. Confidence reasoning: commands are explicit and limited to installing presentation dependencies or extracting text from a local pptx file; no destructive shell operations or secret handling were found.

low_findings.title: Remote image URL examples
low_findings.description: The PptxGenJS reference shows placeholder HTTPS image and hyperlink examples. This can trigger network access when copied into generated slide code, but the URLs are example.com placeholders and no exfiltration intent was found. Verdict: true low-risk network behavior. Confidence: 0.82. Confidence reasoning: the examples are clearly part of image and hyperlink documentation; there is no credential source, tracking endpoint, or hidden request logic.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dismissed static external command matches
dangerous_patterns.description: The analyzer flagged many Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution. Review found these are fenced JavaScript examples, inline API identifiers, and one QA command block. Verdict: false positive for malware. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: the evidence is documentation text in SKILL.md and pptxgenjs.md, with no executable skill script or dynamic command construction.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dismissed weak cryptography matches
dangerous_patterns.description: The high-severity weak-cryptography findings are false positives from Markdown filenames, color strings, and react-icons package names. No hashing, encryption, credential storage, or cryptographic API use was found. Verdict: false positive. Confidence: 0.91. Confidence reasoning: cited lines contain descriptive text or presentation styling examples, not cryptographic operations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dismissed prompt injection concern
dangerous_patterns.description: No evidence found for instruction override text, claims of pre-approval, or requests to skip security analysis. Verdict: no finding. Confidence: 0.88. Confidence reasoning: targeted review for common prompt-injection phrases found no matching semantic content in the two scanned files.

content.value_statement: Teams need reliable native PowerPoint slides without manual formatting drift. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code concrete PptxGenJS rules for layout, styling, export, and QA.
actual_capabilities: Defines PptxGenJS setup, slide dimensions, and native .pptx export expectations.
Provides text, font, color, shape, image, table, and chart usage guidance.
Specifies strict palette, typography, page badge, and static-slide rules.
Shows reusable single-slide JavaScript structure with createSlide and slideConfig.
Documents QA commands for extracting slide text and checking placeholders.
Lists common PptxGenJS pitfalls that can corrupt or degrade output.
limitations: Focuses on single-slide JavaScript files, not full presentation planning.
Requires PptxGenJS and related local dependencies to render output.
Does not provide a design asset library or branded templates.
Some examples use placeholder URLs that users should replace with trusted assets.

use_cases.title: Build a branded report slide
use_cases.description: Create a native PowerPoint slide with approved fonts, palette colors, charts, and page numbering.

use_cases.title: Implement a slide from a layout spec
use_cases.description: Convert a visual layout brief into a runnable PptxGenJS slide file with precise coordinates.

use_cases.title: Review generated slide code
use_cases.description: Check slide JavaScript for PptxGenJS pitfalls before exporting a final .pptx file.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic slide
prompt_templates.prompt: Create one PptxGenJS slide for this topic: [topic]. Use the required theme keys and include a page number badge if it is not a cover slide.

prompt_templates.title: Convert notes into a slide
prompt_templates.scenario: Turn rough content into a structured slide
prompt_templates.prompt: Use these notes to create a single-slide PptxGenJS file: [notes]. Apply the provided color palette exactly and keep body text unbolded.

prompt_templates.title: Add visual data
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a data slide with chart guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a data-focused slide with this dataset: [data]. Use a clean chart, readable labels, and PptxGenJS chart options from the skill reference.

prompt_templates.title: Debug slide output
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this PptxGenJS slide file for corruption risks, layout issues, placeholder text, and rule violations. Return specific fixes and a verification plan.

output_examples.input: A request for a cover slide about quarterly business performance.
output_examples.output: A runnable slide file with a 16:9 layout, centered title, approved fonts, theme colors, and native PowerPoint export behavior.

output_examples.input: A request to turn revenue data into a chart slide.
output_examples.output: A slide plan and implementation using PptxGenJS chart options, readable labels, palette-matched colors, and clean spacing.

output_examples.input: A request to inspect a generated slide before delivery.
output_examples.output: A concise QA checklist covering text extraction, placeholder checks, color rules, page badges, and known PptxGenJS corruption risks.

best_practices: Provide the exact theme palette and slide index before generating code.
Render a preview and extract text before treating the slide as complete.
Use fresh option objects for repeated shapes and shadows.
anti_patterns: Do not use colors outside the provided palette or prefix hex colors with #.
Do not make createSlide asynchronous because the compile script expects synchronous output.
Do not copy placeholder remote URLs into production slides without review.

faq.question: Does this skill create native PowerPoint files?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides PptxGenJS code that exports native .pptx files.

faq.question: Can it build a full deck?
faq.answer: It focuses on single-slide files. A separate workflow can combine generated slides into a deck.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it require internet access?
faq.answer: No for local assets. Some examples show remote images, but trusted local files are safer.

faq.question: Can it use custom brand colors?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the required theme keys and use only those palette values.

faq.question: How should output be checked?
faq.answer: Generate a preview, extract slide text, check placeholders, fix issues, and verify again.
